I'll analyze the contract carefully:

1. Canonicalization rewrites an id to canonical width: left-zero-padded to **minimum of 4 digits**
2. An id already at 4 or more digits is returned unchanged
3. Kind and numeric value never change
4. Only width may change
5. Canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op

## (a) Implementation

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  if x.width >= PAD then x
  else Id(x.kind, x.value, PAD)
}
```

## (b) Lemma with ensures clauses

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= x.width
  ensures x.width >= PAD ==> Canonicalize(x) == x
  ensures x.width < PAD ==> Canonicalize(x).width == PAD
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)
{ }
```

**Explanation of each clause:**

- `kind` is preserved — canonicalization never changes which kind of entity is referred to
- `value` is preserved — the numeric value never changes
- `width >= PAD` — the result always has at least 4 digits of display width
- `width >= x.width` — width never shrinks (an id already wider than 4 stays that way)
- `x.width >= PAD ==> result == x` — if already canonical (width ≥ 4), it's a no-op (identity)
- `x.width < PAD ==> result.width == PAD` — if narrower than 4, width is set to exactly 4
- `Wellformed(result)` — the output is wellformed (width can hold the value), preserved from input since value doesn't change and width only grows
- `Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)` — idempotence: canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op
